title: Another setback for TMC: Mamata loyalist quits party month after appointment as state chief
content: West Bengal's Trinamool Congress unit president Chandrima Bhattacharya has stepped down from all party positions amidst a wave of leaders departing the party. This development follows significant losses in the recent assembly elections and a subsequent organizational restructuring. The TMC has experienced internal divisions, with a notable split and a large contingent of Lok Sabha MPs joining a different party and aligning with the BJP-led NDA.

title: 'Demanded $100,000 for our release': Pak gang rape victim names deputy PM Ishaq Dar's kin
content: A cryptocurrency dispute is now central to the investigation of an alleged kidnapping and gang rape of two foreign women in Pakistan. One victim claims Raza Dar, a relative of the deputy prime minister, demanded $100,000 for their release after they were held captive and sexually assaulted. Investigators are exploring if Dar lured the women to Pakistan for a $1.5 million crypto deal, staging the abduction to appear genuine.

title: 'Absolute power corrupts': Raj Thackeray attacks BJP over 'insensitive' remarks on Mumbai deaths
content: Maharashtra Navnirman Sena chief Raj Thackeray has strongly criticized Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis and the BJP, accusing them of arrogance and shielding colleagues who made insensitive remarks after two rain-related deaths in Mumbai. Thackeray lamented the decline of Maharashtra's political culture, urging Fadnavis to demand resignations from leaders behaving "shamelessly" to uphold the state's traditions.

title: 'I went up to Messi after the game. He said ... ': Vozinha after WC exit
content: After Cape Verde's dramatic 3-2 defeat to Argentina national football team, goalkeeper Vozinha revealed that Lionel Messi hugged him and said, "You are great. Your people should be proud of you." The Cape Verde veteran, who made eight saves, called the exchange one of the most memorable moments of his career.

title: 'Rain only good for those in balconies': Every monsoon, same old story in Mumbai
content: Mumbai's annual monsoon test has begun with the season's first heavy downpour, exposing civic unpreparedness. Tragic incidents, including a boy's death from a fallen tree and a man drowning in an open manhole, have marred the arrival of the rains. Commuters faced disruptions, with social media buzzing about flooded roads and delayed trains, highlighting the city's ongoing struggle with its monsoon challenges.

title: After 8,000 layoffs, Meta CEO Zuckerberg admits AI progress was slower than expected
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg acknowledged internal restructuring hasn't yielded expected results, particularly in AI agent development, admitting executives 'miscalculated' timelines.  Despite significant layoffs and AI investments, progress has been slower than anticipated.  However, Zuckerberg anticipates substantial AI benefits within three to six months.  Separately, the company addressed mouse-tracking software, assuring no employee data was used for AI training and confirming future opt-in policies.

title: Office spaces steal the spotlight as real estate investments hit six-year high
content: The second quarter of 2026 showcased explosive growth in Indian real estate, attracting a staggering $2.9 billion in institutional investments—marking a remarkable 70% increase from the previous year. With a six-year peak now reached, both domestic and international investors exhibited robust confidence, particularly in office and mixed-use properties. Notably, ADIA’s substantial $675 million investment underscored the sector's resilience amid global challenges.

title: Spouse accused of adultery can't invoke right to privacy, says SC
content: The Supreme Court has ruled that the right to privacy under Article 21 cannot shield a man from disclosing mobile call records and hotel stay details to prove adultery. Upholding the Delhi High Court's decision, the apex court stated that privacy is not absolute and can be restricted in public interest, especially when adultery is a ground for divorce under the Hindu Marriage Act.

title: Days after Meta Layoffs, Zuckerberg says companies should empower workers, not replace them
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg believes AI-driven job losses are exaggerated, advocating for tools that boost individual productivity. This comes as the tech giant laid off 8,000 employees, reassigning another 7,000 to AI projects, while scrapping thousands of open roles.  The company is heavily investing in AI infrastructure, aiming for efficiency amidst a competitive landscape.  This stance contrasts with other industry leaders' more cautious outlooks on AI's impact on employment.

title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang to join Elon Musk, Sundar Pichai in space mission
content: Nvidia is ramping up its space computing ambitions with its first orbital data center, Space-1. The tech giant is actively hiring for a System Software Principal Architect to develop software for this new platform, designed to withstand harsh space conditions. This move signals Nvidia's commitment to orbital AI infrastructure, joining other tech giants like SpaceX and Google in exploring space-based connectivity and data centers.

title: Centre puts Ram temple recce operative, Hafiz Saeed's son-in-law on terror list
content: ​​According to the ministry, he carried out reconnaissance of the Ram Janmabhoomi complex in Ayodhya, the RSS headquarters in Nagpur and the Indian Oil refinery in Panipat. He is also accused of serving as the launching commander for the Lasiyakot sector and facilitating infiltration through tunnels while arranging drone deliveries of arms and ammunition into India.

title: Beyond Hormuz: What HPCL Rajasthan Refinery Limited means for India's energy future
content: India is bolstering its energy security following the Strait of Hormuz disruption. Prime Minister Modi inaugurated the HPCL Rajasthan Refinery, a crucial step towards self-reliance for a nation heavily reliant on oil imports. This project, alongside other development initiatives, aims to strengthen manufacturing, boost exports, and enhance resilience against future supply shocks, underscoring India's commitment to national interest and citizen welfare.

title: 'Tougher days will come': Ex-India cricketer's reality check for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
content: Former India wicketkeeper Dinesh Karthik has lauded 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi as a rare T20 talent with the potential to shatter records. Karthik highlighted the youngster's fearless batting and ability to score freely. While acknowledging the immense potential, Karthik also advised Sooryavanshi to stay grounded and learn to manage off-field attention, which he believes will be his biggest challenge.

title: After poor Pro League campaign, India hockey coach says 'capable of beating anyone'
content: India ended the FIH Pro League season in eighth place with 19 points from 16 matches, finishing only above Pakistan, who ended without a point.

title: Punjab man set ablaze by brother-in-law while trying to bring wife home; CCTV captures horror
content: A domestic dispute turned deadly in Tarn Taran when a man, Lovepreet Singh, was allegedly set ablaze by his brother-in-law while trying to bring his wife home. Lovepreet succumbed to his injuries, and his sister-in-law, Gurjit Kaur, who attempted to save him, also died. The accused brother-in-law was injured but rescued. CCTV footage captured the horrific incident, leading to the accused's arrest and the wife's detention.

title: 'They asked for my jersey but kicked the crap out of me on field': Messi after Cape Verde win
content: After leading Argentina national football team to a hard-fought 3-2 win over Cape Verde, Lionel Messi joked that his opponents "kick the crap out of me" before asking for selfies and jerseys. Messi praised Argentina's set-piece improvement but admitted the defending champions struggled to control the game and need to improve.

title: China's Xi promotes two PLA generals after anti-corruption purge thins top military ranks
content: China's leader, Xi Jinping, has made notable advancements by promoting two military officials to generals, marking a pivotal change in the People's Liberation Army's hierarchy. Following rigorous anti-corruption efforts that reshaped the leadership structure, these promotions are essential for restoring loyalty and filling key positions within the Central Military Commission, setting the stage for a comprehensive reorganization prior to the forthcoming Communist Party congress.

title: Did Iran force people to attend Khamenei's funeral in large numbers? What report claims
content: Authorities in Iran are reportedly compelling workers and businesses to participate in funeral ceremonies for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Reports indicate widespread directives for closures and mandatory attendance, with warnings of business seals for non-compliance. Even charitable organizations and restaurants face pressure to contribute, highlighting a significant logistical mobilization for the upcoming events.

title: Days after Ayodhya row, theft of donations and offerings alleged at Badrinath temple
content: Allegations of stolen donations at Badrinath temple have prompted an investigation by the Badri Kedar Temple Committee (BKTC). A social organisation claims a BKTC employee is involved, urging a review of CCTV footage. While the BKTC chairman clarified the employee's role, he assured strict action if irregularities are confirmed. The committee is forming a probe panel to examine evidence and statements, with legal action planned if wrongdoing is found.

title: How High Altitude Pseudo Satellites will give India's military eyes in the sky for weeks
content: India's Air Force is set to boost its surveillance capabilities with the acquisition of High Altitude Pseudo Satellites (HAPS). These advanced drones will operate at 20 km, offering persistent intelligence gathering over vast areas for weeks. Indigenous development is progressing, with DRDO and NAL showcasing successful trials. These solar-powered aircraft promise a significant leap in reconnaissance, bridging the gap between satellites and conventional planes.

title: PM Modi unveils India's 1st greenfield refinery, Rs 1.06L cr projects in Rajasthan
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated a new terminal at Jodhpur Airport and launched the Modified UDAN scheme. In Balotra, he dedicated projects worth Rs 1.06 lakh crore, including India's first greenfield integrated refinery-cum-petrochemical complex. He also handed over appointment letters to 54,000 Rajasthan government employees, emphasizing the government's commitment to project completion and self-reliance.

title: Year after calling India 'dead economy', Trump says country 'doing very well at 7-8%'
content: US President Donald Trump has lauded India's robust economic growth, a significant departure from his past criticisms. He cited India's 7-8% growth as an example of strong performance that shouldn't be stifled by high interest rates. This shift comes as India and the US are nearing a bilateral trade agreement, with negotiations gaining momentum.

title: India eyes $1 trillion exports milestone: Which sectors will drive growth?
content: During the April-June quarter, exports saw a 15% growth in merchandise shipments and around 11% in services exports. Which sectors have contributed to exports growth? What role has the PLI scheme played and will trade deals help expedite growth? We decode:

title: 'Trump is your president': White house 'inserts' itself into Taylor-Travis' wedding
content: The White House playfully spoofed Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding announcement, altering a New York City billboard to read "Trump is your president." This social media jab follows Donald Trump's own Eras Tour-inspired graphic and his history of public criticism towards Swift, particularly after her endorsement of Kamala Harris. Despite past harsh words, Trump had offered a more positive sentiment regarding the couple's engagement.

title: Tiny coffin beside Khamenei's: 14-month-old granddaughter honoured at funeral
content: Iran mourned its late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei with funeral ceremonies in Tehran, where his coffin was displayed alongside that of his 14-month-old granddaughter, killed in an Israeli airstrike.  The event also honored other family members, including his son-in-law and daughter.  Thousands gathered for prayers, with traditional practices observed.  The funeral procession is expected to span several days and locations, including neighboring Iraq.

title: 'You have to communicate clearly': Ex-India keeper issues warning over Sooryavanshi
content: Former wicketkeeper Parthiv Patel advocates for clear communication with 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi regarding his international debut. While acknowledging Sooryavanshi's potential, Patel stresses the need for the team management to explain his current position in a squad featuring established players like Sanju Samson and Abhishek Sharma. Bowling coach Morne Morkel echoed this sentiment, emphasizing support for consistent performers and a reluctance to disrupt the batting order solely for a new inclusion.

title: Mumbai schools, colleges closed today as IMD issues red alert for heavy rain
content: Mumbai faces a red alert with extremely heavy rainfall expected Saturday, July 4. Schools and colleges are closed for the afternoon session as a precaution. Residents are urged to stay indoors due to intense showers, gusty winds up to 60 kmph, and a high tide warning. Authorities advise avoiding travel and monitoring official updates.

title: Govt cracks down on Telegram over pirated movies, OTT content, seeks report in 15 days
content: Government cracks down on Telegram over rampant piracy of films and OTT content, issuing a 15-day ultimatum for improved safety filters and an action report. Additionally, access to the messaging app is restricted across India until June 22 to prevent cheating in the NEET-UG re-examination, following arrests of a gang involved in exam malpractice.

title: Bengaluru crèche horror: Parents say kid has loo phobia after assault; nanny arrested
content: Parents report disturbing behavioral changes in toddlers after alleged abuse at a Capgemini campus daycare. A two-and-a-half-year-old girl developed an intense fear of bathrooms, allegedly due to being locked inside. While some caretakers are arrested, investigations continue into harsh treatment and low salaries. The Home Minister criticized Capgemini's oversight, impacting Bengaluru's image.

title: Cabo Verde give Messi ultimate scare as they sign off in style
content: Day 23 saw the Blue Sharks almost stunning Messi and Co, King Salah reach the Round of 16 and Colombia win the Powder Derby. Read more in today's Offside.

title: 'Why is everyone pointing the gun at me?': Ex-India selector defends Sanju Samson
content: Calls for 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's India debut intensify after a record IPL season, but former selector Saba Karim urges patience. Karim defended Sanju Samson's current place, highlighting his match-winning form. He emphasized that Sooryavanshi's inclusion in the senior squad is a significant learning opportunity, allowing him to develop in a competitive environment before earning his playing XI spot.

title: Mumbai-Ahmedabad bullet train to get operational by 2027; check features, time & more
content: India's first bullet train is set to begin its journey by 2027, with the Surat to Bilimora section slated for inauguration. Union Railway Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw announced that construction on the Mumbai-Ahmedabad corridor has surpassed 80% completion. This ambitious project, utilizing Japanese Shinkansen technology, aims to drastically reduce travel time between key cities, paving the way for future high-speed rail expansion across the nation.

title: Government fixing polls with EC's help: INDIA bloc to CJI
content: A united opposition has approached the Chief Justice of India, alleging that the current government is manipulating elections with a "biased" Election Commission. They've urged the suspension of electoral roll updates, citing concerns over EVMs and a desire to reinstate ballot papers. The INDIA bloc claims poll manipulation in recent elections and fears institutional failure, questioning where citizens can turn if even the judiciary cannot resolve these issues.

title: 'Wedding that would never happen': Siya Goyal's 'Snapchat message' to friend goes viral
content: A viral Snapchat screenshot allegedly shows Siya Goyal requesting Aadhaar card details for non-existent wedding tickets, days after she and Chetan Chaudhary were remanded to judicial custody for the murder of Pune realtor Ketan Agarwal. The couple is accused of pushing Agarwal, Goyal's fiancé, off a cliff. Police are examining coded chats and a second recovered mobile phone, with a key witness now coming forward.

title: Egypt head coach raises Palestinian flag after historic FIFA World Cup win
content: Egypt's historic FIFA World Cup Round of 32 victory over Australia was marked by an emotional tribute from coach Hossam Hassan, who raised the Palestinian flag and dedicated the win to both nations.  The Pharaohs secured their first-ever knockout stage advancement via a dramatic penalty shootout after a 1-1 draw. They now face Argentina or Cape Verde in the Round of 16.

title: Who is Neil? The 1-tonne elephant seal and Tasmania's 'reigning troublemaker' winning hearts across social media
content: A 1000 kg elephant seal named Neil has become an unlikely internet sensation on Australia's southern coast. Known for his 'troublemaker' antics like blocking roads and damaging property, Neil's behavior is actually normal for his age as he practices for mating rituals. While his online fame is soaring, officials urge the public to maintain distance, highlighting the real danger lies in humans approaching the massive animal.

title: Opinion: Congress At Akal Takht: A New Chapter But Not Historical Closure
content: The Congress high command may have achieved an organisational accommodation on paper, but the real challenge will be converting this arrangement into collective leadership

title: Tech Layoffs In 1st Half Of 2026 Outpace Entire 2025, India Second-Worst Hit
content: AI-related tech layoffs worldwide touched 1.28 lakh employees by July 1, 2026, higher than the 1.25 lakh layoffs recorded during the whole of 2025.

title: J&K School Book Calls Terrorists "Great Personalities", Alleges Civil Group
content: The book, cleared under Samagra Shiksha, turns history on its head by celebrating anti-national figures as role models, the JKPF said

title: 'Wedding That Won't Happen': Siya Goyal's Snapchat Message Emerges
content: Police requested custody citing face-to-face interrogation of Siya Goyal and Chetan Chaudhary was necessary to unravel the meaning of the conversation between them

title: 'Dehradun Now Virtually Part Of NCR': Pushkar Dhami Flaunts New Expressway
content: Pushkar Singh Dhami has become only the second chief minister in Uttarakhand to complete five years in office

title: "Avenge Blood Of Our Leader": Mourners Gather For Ali Khamenei's Funeral
content: The mourners, some in tears, made their way through strict security towards the courtyard where Khamenei's coffin was placed for people to pay their respects.

title: What's Holding Delhi Back? Housing, Water Among Key Challenges Identified
content: The issues came under the spotlight at a high-level meeting chaired by Lieutenant Governor TS Sandhu, where officials discussed a long-term development blueprint aimed at shaping the Capital's future growth.

title: UK Man Was Assaulted By Attackers. Cops Arrested Him Instead As Accused Fled
content: A clip of the footage was shared by Reform UK's Shadow Chancellor and Newark MP Robert Jenrick, who criticised the Birmingham Police in multiple posts.

title: 2 Elephant Tusks, 133 Illegally Sawn Sal Timber Seized In Assam, 2 Arrested
content: One of the accused also claimed that the tusks were collected after an elephant had died naturally inside a forest.

title: "Trump's Still Your President": White House Mocks Taylor Swift Wedding
content: The White House's official social media accounts shared a series of posts that appeared to take inspiration from Swift's music and branding.

title: Chandrababu Naidu And Jagan Reddy's Parties In Credit War Over Steel Plant
content: Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu described the JSW Steel plant as a landmark for Rayalaseema's future and said his government had removed all hurdles that had stalled the investment

title: A Rs 7,000 Crore Push: Not Just Devotees, Investors Are Heading To Vrindavan Too
content: Taken together, initiatives place Vrindavan region close to the Rs 7,000 crore mark in planned and executing infrastructure signals.

title: Over 8,000 Civilians Killed In Ukrainian Strikes Since War Broke Out: Russia
content: From February 2022 to June 30, 2026, the total number of casualties reached 30,913, including about 8,434 fatalities

title: "Don't Know If I Look Good": Trump On Not Wearing Bathing Suit
content: Speaking with Usha Vance on "Storytime with the Second Lady," as America celebrated its 250th birthday, the billionaire property tycoon opened with his favorite topic -- real estate, of course.

title: PM Modi Launches India's First Greenfield Refinery-Petrochemical Hub In Rajasthan
content: The state-of-the-art complex integrates refining and petrochemical production, with a petrochemical capacity of 2.4 MMTPA.

title: Bengaluru Daycare Horror Whistleblower Arrested For Circulating Abuse Videos
content: According to the police, the woman who brought the alleged abuse to light "leaked the sensitive videos", and so an action is being taken against her.

title: DMK MLA Anitha Radhakrishnan gets bail in case over remarks against T.N. CM Vijay
content: After his arrest on Friday, Mr. Radhakrishnan was produced before the court in Tiruchendur, at around 10.30 p.m.

title: Experts discuss One Health at two-day conference in Chennai
content: According to a release, the conference provided a vital platform for multi-stakeholder dialogue among policymakers, scientists, public health experts, biosecurity specialists, veterinarians, and academics

title: Absence of senior leaders at Kadapa steel plant inauguration raises eyebrowsvdvd
content: Former district unit President, and his spouse, a Kadapa MLA’s absence at JSW Rayalaseema Steel Plant inauguration raises speculation about future alignments

title: Assam documents butterflies of Bodoland Territorial Region
content: The 3,653 sq. km. forest landscape in areas under a Sixth Schedule council supports 346 recorded species, including several rare, endemic, and threatened taxa

title: Kerala Minister orders probe into Achencoil tribal family’s distress; local ST promoter removed
content: Following assessment of the family’s plight, Minister K.A. Thulsai directs SC/ST welfare department to submit urgent report on the matter

title: Bandra is disappearing: Is this what Mumbai calls development?
content: The suburb is changing fast. With redevelopment, Mumbai will likely metamorphose into a city of identical towers instead of distinct neighbourhoods

title: Monsoon session of Parliament from July 20: Kiren Rijiju
content: “The Session will commence on 20 July, 2026 and continue till 13 August, 2026 for meaningful debate, discussion and decisions on issues of national importance,” Kiren Rijiju said

title: No need for India to take a leaf out of Pakistan’s Iran mediation book, says envoy
content: Mediating in an “already crowded” field on the Iran crisis would not have benefited India, said Ambassador to China Vikram Doraiswami

title: Kasaragod to figure in new AIIMS proposal, MP tells collective
content: The assurance from the State government came during a meeting between Rajmohan Unnithan,MP, and Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan, says AIIMS Kasaragod People’s Collective.

title: Centre’s intervention to stabilise Andhra mango sector | Explained
content: Andhra Pradesh is India’s largest mango-producing State, with the crop cultivated over nearly five lakh hectares and an annual production of close to 40 lakh tonnes in a normal year

title: Monsoon tracker July 4 LIVE: IMD issues red alert in five districts in Odisha
content: Incessant and heavy rains threw life out of gear in Mumbai and its metropolitan region; the monsoon is active in Gujarat with “exceptional heavy rainfall” in Junagadh, where a few areas witnessed severe waterlogging

title: Fifty Bangladeshi nationals from Attur detention camp sent to West Bengal by train for deportation
content: The deportees, comprising 44 men and six women, were previously lodged at the special detention camp located within the Attur taluk office complex in Salem district

title: After Operation Sindoor, trust in ‘made-in-India’ defence platforms got enhanced: Rajnath Singh
content: Rajnath Singh reiterated that "today, our defence production has reached over ₹1.78 lakh crore, and about 8-9 years ago, it stood at approximately ₹46,000 crore"

title: Andhra Pradesh to set up world-class Sports Centre of Excellence with JSW support: Minister Nara Lokesh
content: He also announced that the government had released ₹2.23 crore for the training of 18 athletes selected for this year’s Asian Games, Commonwealth Games and Para Commonwealth Games

title: Karuvannur bank fraud case: CPI(M) leaders A.C. Moideen, K. Radhakrishnan, M.M. Varghese get bail
content: Bail granted by Special Court for Prevention of Money Laundering Act in Kochi. The CPI(M) leaders, among the additional accused, had filed objections and contended that allegations in complaint were not sufficient to proceed against them for offence under Section 4 of PMLA

title: Goa Governor Pusapati Ashok Gajapathi Raju raises objection over construction of YSRCP office in Vizianagaram’s Dasannapeta
content: Goa Governor Pusapati Ashok Gajapathi Raju alleged that the officials tampered records and showed his family property as government lands, and the Opposition defends the allocation

title: Sleepless fans in Kerala put through wringer as Argentina goes to the brink and back
content: The close contest highlighted Argentina’s vulnerabilities, leaving fans across the state in a frenzy as they celebrated their team’s narrow escape from elimination.

title: Karuvannur bank case: court accepts ED’s supplementary chargesheet against CPI(M), party leaders
content: Special Court for Prevention of Money Laundering Act set to issue summons to those named in chargesheet and direct them to appear on July 4

title: Operative behind recce of Ram temple and RSS HQ, Hafiz Saeed's relative in designated terrorist list
content: All 23 of them were designated as terrorist under fourth schedule of the Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act (UAPA)

title: As monsoon rains falter, Wayanad farmers in Kerala fear poor harvest
content: In Wayanad, rainfall deficit has adversely affected agriculture. The district received only 270.23 mm of rain in June, the lowest for the month in the past five years, according to Hume Centre for Ecology and Wildlife Biology

title: Mamata Banerjee faces fresh jolt as TMC Bengal chief Chandrima Bhattacharya resigns
content: In a letter addressed to the Trinamool Congress chairperson, Chandrima Bhattacharya also resigned from all other posts she held in the party, triggering speculations on whether she had severed her ties with the party for all practical purposes.

title: Telangana Govt approves recruitment for 6,424 posts in Police dept, including SI and Constable jobs
content: Telangana Government issued two orders to fill the posts, directs TPRB to issue recruitment notifications and schedules without delay

title: Mumbai BJP chief faces flak over remarks on monsoon-related deaths in city
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) MP Sanjay Raut shared a screenshot of Mr. Satam’s viral video. "Merchants of death. The cruel face of Mumbai BJP," he said in a message on X

title: More Chinese investment good for larger relationship, says India’s envoy to China
content: Ambassador to China Vikram Doraiswami made a pitch for greater Indian exports to China, especially in areas such as pharmaceuticals where India is globally competitive

title: Ram Temple Trust meeting on July 6; SIT report, post of CEO part of agenda
content: The meeting is happening amid two parallel investigations into the allegations, one being conducted by a Special Investigation Team, whose tenure has been extended till July end, and another by the police.

title: Adani-MSC Vizhinjam port stake transfer: Pinarayi Vijayan says Opposition to challenge legally and politically
content: Opposition Leader asks Kerala government to ‘stop acting as a spokesperson’ for Adani Group and instead take immediate steps to safeguard the State’s interests

title: West Bengal: IMD issues red alert for heavy rains as low pressure develops over Bay of Bengal
content: As the weather system moved closer to the West Bengal-Odisha coast, the India Meteorological Department issued a red warning — the highest level of colour-coded warning — for South 24 Parganas and Purba Medinipur district

title: PM Modi inaugurates new Jodhpur airport terminal, launches Modified UDAN scheme
content: PM Modi arrived in Jodhpur and was received at the airport by Governor Haribhau Bagade, Union Minister and Jodhpur MP Gajendra Singh Shekhawat, and Chief Minister Bhajanlal Sharma

title: Coimbatore-based textile research body plans to promote green processing through ₹80-crore project
content: With Western buyers looking at garment suppliers who adopt sustainable practices, SITRA has mooted a project for green textile processing

title: MIB steps up pressure on Telegram; seeks proactive action against piracy
content: The Ministry of Information and Broadcasting has demanded that Telegram proactively take action on piracy groups and channels on its platform

title: Barun Biswas' family approaches CM for fresh SIT probe into his murder
content: The demand comes a day ahead of the 14th death anniversary of Biswas, who was shot dead by assailants near Gobardanga railway station in North 24 Parganas district on July 5, 2012

title: T.N. BJP urges Governor Arlekar to act against participation of ‘two private individuals’ in Cabinet meeting
content: Speaking to the media, BJP State chief Nainar Nagenthran stated: “In the recently concluded Cabinet meeting, apart from the ministers, two external individuals — John Arokiasamy and Vishnu Reddy — also participated. We have submitted a petition to the Governor urging strict action on this matter.”

title: Heavy rains batter several parts of Kerala; riverbank residents put on alert
content: Rains lead to rise in water levels in many rivers and dams, inundation in low-lying areas, and damage to property, prompting authorities to issue alerts and precautionary measures

title: Green March in Ballari promotes year-round commitment to environmental conservation
content: Vanamahotsava 2026: District administration, police, Forest Department and students join awareness march, urging citizens to plant and nurture trees as part of everyday life

title: AMMA crisis: Shwetha Menon defends her presidency, claims support from superstars
content: ‘Those who wish to be part of the AMMA committee should do so by contesting elections as we did, rather than attempt entry through the back door,’ she says in a Facebook post

title: No full runway shutdown at Srinagar airport this year: airport authorities
content: The airport added that the previously proposed NOTAM regarding full runway closure on Mondays and Tuesdays has been withdrawn

title: AMMA crisis: Women actors accuse Shwetha Menon of communal polarisation, political and corporate nexus
content: Actors Ansiba Hassan, Maala Parvathy, and Usha Haseena address the media hours after Shwetha Menon secured a stay from a munsif court against functioning of AMMA ad-hoc committee that followed her resignation announcement

title: Manipur CM attends BJP MLA Valte’s last rites, regrets not being by his side during 2023 attack
content: I came here to pay my last respects to my friend Pu Valte. To be able to attend it is a huge honour for me. Valte’s family is like my family. His friends and close ones are my friends too, says Manipur Chief Minister Y Khemchand Singh

title: Do not see India-Peru FTA happening anytime soon: Piyush Goyal
content: Minister Piyush Goyal said a team of Indian officials will be visiting Canada for the next round of negotiations

title: MHA designates 23 Pakistan-based individuals as ‘terrorists’ under UAPA
content: Including the names of terrorists in the list will allow the NIA to block their finances, impose embargo on arms sales, and seize their assets

title: NHRC seeks action from RPF chief on harassment of rights activist
content: Guwahati-based Monojit Singha says he was victimised after he exposed an illegal railway quarter-rent racket

title: Assault case: T.N. Minister Marie Wilson told to appear before Puducherry court without fail on July 10
content: Mr. Wilson was supposed to appear before the court on Saturday to receive the additional charge sheet. However, his advocate informed the court of his inability to appear due to his Ministerial responsibilities

title: Assam district orders deportation of man declared foreigner
content: Adjudged a non-citizen by a Foreigners’ Tribunal, Kayam Ansari has been asked to exit India under the provisions of the Immigrants (Expulsion from Assam) Act, 1950

title: Delhi CM Rekha Gupta orders rollback of work from home, staggered office timings
content: With the geopolitical situation normalising, Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta has approved the withdrawal of work from home on Wednesday and Saturday

title: Day 15 of CJP protest: Fasting Wangchuk's health worsens; 'Why Pradhan not sacked yet,' Dipke asks
content: In another post, Mr. Dipke said the government would be responsible if anything happened to the activist

title: From Aldur to orbit: The remarkable journey of Chikkamagaluru boy-turned-Pixxel CEO
content: Hailing from Aldur in Chikkamagaluru, Awais Ahmed is among the leading young entrepreneurs in India’s private space sector

title: Battery apps taken down in India after stalled e-rickshaw rides
content: Over the last few days, videos of people using Chinese battery management apps to remotely cut power to e-rickshaws mid-ride have gone viral

title: AI hiring outpaces overall IT recruitment in India, report shows
content: The sector’s AI hiring rose 16% year-on-year in June, while overall IT jobs ‌declined 3%, according to job portal Naukri’s monthly JobSpeak report

title: Absenteeism crackdown sparks calls for systemic reforms in T.N. public health sector
content: Government doctors say pay disparities, manpower shortage, extended duty hours, and workplace safety are some of the issues that need to be addressed. They also accuse successive governments of finding it politically convenient to position doctors against the public
